title: Training continuously-coupled reconfigurable photonic chips with quantum machine learning
authors: Denis Stanev, Nicolò Spagnolo, Fabio Sciarrino
abstract: Integrated photonic technologies have recently shown significant advances, enabling the possibility to implement reconfigurable interferometers with increasing size. One of the main tasks to fully exploit the capabilities of reconfigurable integrated interferometers is the possibility to precisely program their operation to perform a desired target unitary. While recipes are known for circuit layouts based on a cascade of beam-splitter and phase-shifter operations, a methodology applicable for reconfigurable continuously-coupled waveguide arrays is currently missing. Here, we devise a machine learning based approach for this task, using a black box methodology that does not rely on precise a-priori modeling of the circuit internal architectures. We verify the effectiveness and the robustness of this approach via numerical simulations on different continuously-coupled waveguides layouts, either with planar or 3D structures. The proposed method makes use of a limited number of single- and two-photon measurements, making it suitable for optical quantum information processing. The obtained results open the perspective of employing this methodology as an effective tool to program the operation of integrated interferometers designed via different architectures.
summary: The paper presents a machine learning method to program reconfigurable photonic chips made of coupled waveguides to perform specific quantum operations. By using a black-box approach based on photon measurements, the method avoids the need for complex internal modeling. This provides a versatile tool for controlling various photonic architectures for quantum information processing.
main_problem: Developing a methodology to precisely program reconfigurable continuously-coupled waveguide arrays to perform a desired target unitary without needing a-priori knowledge of the internal circuit architecture.
main_result: The authors demonstrate a robust, black-box machine learning approach that can effectively train photonic chips to implement target unitaries using only a limited number of single- and two-photon measurements.
method: A machine learning-based black-box approach that utilizes single- and two-photon measurements to optimize the operation of the photonic circuit.
model_or_system: Reconfigurable integrated photonic chips consisting of continuously-coupled waveguide arrays, including both planar and 3D architectures.
key_observables: Single-photon and two-photon measurements used to verify the target unitary implementation.
main_assumptions: The method assumes a black-box setting where the internal architecture of the circuit does not need to be precisely modeled a-priori.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of programming waveguide arrays, proposes a machine learning-based black-box methodology, and verifies its effectiveness and robustness through numerical simulations on various planar and 3D waveguide layouts.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant for quantum optics and quantum information as it provides a scalable way to control complex photonic integrated circuits, which are essential for optical quantum computing and quantum networks.
